title: poa arctica subsp. grayana
intro: Poa arctica subsp. grayana, also known as Arctic Bluegrass, is a subspecies of grass that is native to the Arctic tundra regions of North America. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, the plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its medicinal value. The health benefits of Poa arctica subsp. grayana include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory health, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
